name: Walk the Plank & Save the cat

reviews.review_description:
A fun and creative VR experience that delivers exactly what it promises! Walk the Plank & Save the Cat is a great mix of tension and light-hearted gameplay, making the most of VR’s ability to play with height and fear. Stepping out onto the plank genuinely gives you that uneasy feeling, while the “save the cat” element adds a nice, playful objective.

It’s easy to jump into and perfect for showing off VR to friends — especially watching their reactions! While it may not have the deepest gameplay or long-term replay value, it absolutely shines as a quick, entertaining experience.  You did need some space to play and I did find on the roller coaster it was a bit glitchy with frame rate, that said -
Simple, effective, and good fun — a solid 4-star VR title.

Save The Cat – Plank Rescue Experience delivers the classic VR plank sensation with welcome variety across multiple levels. Your goal is to calm the cat by bringing it food, then carefully rescue it while standing on a plank high above the city — a true test of nerves.

Both VR and Mixed Reality modes are available, adding flexibility and immersion. The pirate ship plank is especially fun, and the recently added roller coaster is a great surprise that adds even more excitement. A tense yet charming experience that’s perfect for thrill-seekers and for showing VR to friends.

First of all the game looks good.
When u walk back over the plank u make the step up in real life.
So that part is done good, you mind is thinking your walking a plank on a high area.

In the beginning it was hard to pick up the cat.
Dont now how to do it, but when you do its easy.

Overal the game
Need some polishing, but overall its a 9/10
Fun to do when u have some spare time.
Try it......save the cat!

No previous VR experience required, perfect for a family gathering with kids or people that want to experience VR for the first time. Both the rollercoaster and the cat plank rescue are very amusing, we played the game with kids and it was a hit.

If you lik richies plank but you feel the lack of accomplishment. then this might be better for you. Now hou have a reason to walk the plank. No cake or telephone but a cat to rescue. This idea is good. What i dont like - and im sue theyll work on it - is that the cat feels like a toy. Even if you drop it it makes no sound ( it should scream). I also noticed in mr if you drop the cat you cant redo or anything. You need to quit and restart the game. Minor issues that will probably be fixed.

reviews.review_title: Superb idea, but still needs work
reviews.review_description:
Despite me having loads of space in my room,it’s pot luck where the lift/plank is placed and sometimes it’s out of my boundary so the game cannot be played properly. It would be good if the lift and the plank could be placed where the player wants it like I have experienced on some other similar games. (Richie’s plank/walk the plank etc)  

My next issue is the cat. When the stars align and the lift and plank are accessible, I just cannot seem to lift and save the cat. I’ve tried all sorts. I’ve seen other reviews have this similar issue, but eventually manage to get the knack. I still haven’t learnt this yet, and just end up flinging the cat off the plank which devastates me. 

All in all, it’s a great concept, great visuals, I just hope the few issues I’ve mentioned above can be ironed out as currently this game is unplayable for me.

AN UNUSUAL MISSION AT DIZZYING HEIGHTS
The adrenaline rush is immediate. Saving a cat at the end of a suspended plank—now that's something out of the ordinary.
Save the Cat – Plank Rescue Experience is truly original: the mechanics are simple yet incredibly effective. 
You stand at the end of a plank suspended in mid-air, hone your balance, and then race to a cat in distress.
The VR immersion is real: the void is believable, the challenge is palpable, and the "save an animal" aspect provides a gentle emotional motivation that contrasts beautifully with the vertigo.
Visually, it's stunning. The environments, the height effects, and the shadows create a breathtaking atmosphere. And the cat models are simply magnificent! 
Still in early access, expect a rather short session; the content is limited and the gameplay is short, for now.
But it's a fascinating VR and MR experience with hand tracking, perfect for testing the headset, experiencing vertigo, and offering a truly unique adventure. 
This title can become a must-have for a quick and intense session by adding variations, levels, and extra challenges.

reviews.review_title: I really want to like this app
reviews.review_description: Unfortunately, I have been trying to get to the 50th floor and no matter what I do to change the boundaries, play outside in my backyard, or indoors  I can't get the boundaries to include the elevator levels. What's interesting is I can press the button to purchase a different game but I can't select the button to leave a review or press the button to get to the 5oth floor.  The graphics are nice and I love the atmosphere better than Walk the Plank.  When this app works properly I will come back and leave an updated review.

Save the Cat VR is a charming and heartwarming experience that blends light puzzle-solving with the joy of immersive interaction. The concept is simple yet surprisingly engaging — find and rescue adorable cats scattered across a series of creative environments. 

The physics feel natural and responsive, making it easy to reach, climb, and carry your rescued feline friends. The collection of the cat initially seems impossible, but after a while it makes sense! Maybe this can be tweaked. The different levels in VR mode offer a nice variety of scenarios, ensuring that gameplay stays fun and never repetitive. Hopefully more levels to come.

One suggestion for improvement would be in XR mode: it would be great if the “Congratulations” message appeared after a short delay. That would give players time to gently place the cat down into their real-world surroundings before the game concludes — a small change that would make the mixed re my ality experience feel even more immersive and satisfying.

Overall, Save the Cat VR is a delightful mix of warmth, creativity, and clever level design that truly shines in virtual reality.

Game has great potential

This game is so fun much fun. I played it with the family and we all laughed and had a blast.   It's not a hard game but it is challenging which is great. I highly recommend this game. All ages will enjoy it.

reviews.review_title: A short but punchy VR thrill
reviews.review_description:
Stepping onto the plank to save a cat sounds simple but it's not. The height sells the illusion, palms get sweaty, and the cat’s reactions are adorably rewarding when you reach it. I love the Mixed reality mode adds a fu. Content is light for now and could use polish, but as a quick challenge for friends and family, it absolutely does the job.
Pay attention - you need space for this game!

Fun game for friends. Had friends over and some people were like it’s just a game and then they get up there and can’t do it cause they scared is hilarious. Plank on the top of the city go an see if u brave enough to walk over and save the cat.  Good time. Good game.
I have fun playing this game. Sometimes it's hard to actually pick up the cat, but the experience itself is really nice to play.
Walking on the plank with a shark swimming around was such a thrilling experience. It was scary, but I absolutely loved it!

Clearly this is inspired by Richie’s plank experience, no one is trying to hide that. But that isn’t available on the quest store any more, and this is. It does require a decent amount of space to play in, and I had some issues picking up the cat, but it is pretty impressive, and is priced right, for what it is. The rollercoaster is a nice inclusion, I was surprised by all of the giant creatures that passed by on the ride.

Unfortunately game requires more than 4 meters free space for you to be able to physically move, and walk the plank. Don't have as much space so cannot test if fully, as i am not able to reach the cat. Besides that, game looks simple but fun. Graphics could be better.

Amazing fun for the family watching my teenagers play is is brilliant they love we have had a game night with it great immersion and graphics credit to the developer will continue to play and wait for future updates
This is the perfect game to Show VR to your gradma or people who are new to vr. You walk on a blank high on top of a skyscraper to rescue a cute cat. A cool experience 🙂
I have not been able to save the cat yet because it’s terrifying but I will keep trying!
